rationale: Both candidates independently surface the latent gap (the untouched hunyuan_image3_transformer.py diffusion loader still calling the removed get_cache_scale API), but X classifies it correctly as a major/blocking correctness bug with a concrete fix (add to _STALE_API_FILES, use the mapper), while Y buries the same finding as a 'non-blocking' comment under an 'APPROVE, no blockers' verdict — inconsistent given the gap later became a real issue (#4891) — and adds an unverifiable claim that it was 'already addressed on main.' X also more directly echoes ground-truth reviewer points (explicitly recommends keeping the regression test permanently, flags that fakes don't exercise a real quant config/checkpoint), whereas Y's test critique focuses only on the delegated-loader path and misses the direct-loader real-checkpoint concern the human reviewer raised. Both are well-grounded in the diff/test code, but X's severity calibration and closer coverage of ground-truth concerns make it the stronger review.
